title: taxillus coriaceus
intro: Taxillus coriaceus, also known as the Chinese gall, is a type of hemiparasitic plant that belongs to the family Santalaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, Taxillus coriaceus is often dried and used as a decoction or made into a tincture, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
